heading: General powers of Commission
label: 3612a General powers of Commission

children.heading: Corporate seal
children.label: a Corporate seal
children.text: The Commission may adopt, alter, and use a corporate seal, which shall be judicially noticed.

children.heading: Bylaws
children.label: b Bylaws
children.text: The Commission may by action of the Board of Directors adopt, amend, and repeal bylaws governing the conduct of its general business and the performance of the powers and duties granted to or imposed upon it by law.

children.heading: Suits by and against Commission
children.label: c Suits by and against Commission

children.text: the amenability of the Commission to suit is limited by Article VIII of the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977, , and otherwise by law;section 3761 of this title

an attachment, garnishment, or similar process may not be issued against salaries or other moneys owed by the Commission to its employees except as provided by and sections 659, 661, and 662 of title 42, or as otherwise specifically authorized by the laws of the United States; andsection 5520a of title 51See References in Text note below.1
the Commission is exempt from the payment of interest on claims and judgments.

children.heading: Contracts, leases, agreements, or other transactions
children.label: d Contracts, leases, agreements, or other transactions
children.text: The Commission may enter into contracts, leases, agreements, or other transactions.
may determine the character of, and necessity for, its obligations and expenditures and the manner in which they shall be incurred, allowed, and paid; and
may incur, allow, and pay its obligations and expenditures, subject to pertinent provisions of law generally applicable to Government corporations.

children.heading: Priority in payment of debts out of bankrupt estates
children.label: f Priority in payment of debts out of bankrupt estates
children.text: The Commission shall have the priority of the Government of the United States in the payment of debts out of bankrupt estates.

The Commission may appoint any United States citizen to have the general powers of a notary public to perform, on behalf of Commission employees and their dependents outside the United States, any notarial act that a notary public is required or authorized to perform within the United States. Unless an earlier expiration is provided by the terms of the appointment, any such appointment shall expire three months after the Canal Transfer Date.

Every notarial act performed by a person acting as a notary under paragraph (1) shall be as valid, and of like force and effect within the United States, as if executed by or before a duly authorized and competent notary public in the United States.

The signature of any person acting as a notary under paragraph (1), when it appears with the title of that person’s office, is prima facie evidence that the signature is genuine, that the person holds the designated title, and that the person is authorized to perform a notarial act.

children.heading: Limitations on authority
children.label: h Limitations on authority
children.text: The authority of the Commission under this section and is subject to the Panama Canal Treaty of 1977 and related agreements, and to chapter 91 of title 31.section 3612b of this title
